name: Chocolate Layer Cake with Date Filling
recipeIngredient: 2 tsp baking powder (1/2 cup plus 1/2 cup)
2 tbsp chopped black walnuts or pecans
1 pound confectioners' sugar (plus 2 tbsp. cocoa powder)
recipeInstructions:
Serves 12
"The date filling makes this special, and the frosting is unusually good.
Because the cake has a custard filling, it needs to be kept refrigerated."
Preheat oven to 350°F. Melt the chocolate in the top of a double boiler over barely simmering water (or in the microwave). Sift the flour, baking powder, and salt together into a separate bowl.
In a large mixing bowl, cream the butter and sugar until fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Blend in the melted chocolate and vanilla extract.
Add the dry ingredients to the chocolate mixture, alternating with the buttermilk, beginning and ending with the flour. Mix gently; do not overbeat.
Pour the batter into three 8-inch buttered cake layer pans. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, or until the cakes have shrunk away a bit from the sides of the pans and the tops spring back when touched with your finger. Allow to stand in pans for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a rack to cool.
While the cakes are cooling, prepare the filling:
1. Heat milk and chopped dates in a double boiler over hot water until the mixture is scalded.
2. Beat an egg white, then add sugar, flour, and salt; beat again.
3. Add half of the hot date mixture to the egg white mixture, beating well, then stir back into the pan and cook for 3 minutes, stirring with a rubber spatula, until thick.
4. Remove from heat and let cool before adding nuts and vanilla extract. Refrigerate for 1-1/2 hours.
Prepare the frosting:
1. Combine all frosting ingredients in a large mixing bowl.
2. Beat on high speed for 1 minute, or until light and creamy.
Assemble: Place one cake layer on a serving plate and cover to within 1/4 inch of the edge with half the chilled filling. Top with another cake layer and remaining filling. Finish with the last cake layer. Frost the sides and top of the cake with fudge frosting, forming swirls with a knife. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
